questions.question: Explain the Android application sandbox security mechanism and its importance in mobile security.
expectedPoints: Uses dedicated user IDs (UID) for process isolation
Provides separate file system spaces for each app
Forms foundation of Android's security model

questions.question: Describe three common Android security vulnerabilities from OWASP Mobile Top 10 and explain how to prevent each one.
expectedPoints: M2: Insecure Data Storage - Use Android KeyStore, encrypted storage, avoid world-readable files
M3: Insecure Communication - Implement HTTPS with certificate pinning, validate certificates
M5: Insufficient Cryptography - Use proven algorithms, avoid hardcoded keys, proper key management
M1: Improper Platform Usage - Correct use of Intents, proper biometric implementation
General prevention: Follow secure coding standards, regular security testing
